skill.name: memory-init
skill.description: Initializes a project-local memory system by creating assistant instruction files, a long-term memory file, and a daily memory folder.
skill.summary: Set up project-local memory files for Claude, Claude Code, and Codex workflows.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command, hidden file, weak cryptography, and entropy patterns. Review found no malicious intent, no network access, and no prompt injection attempt; the weak cryptography and entropy alerts are false positives from readable markdown content. The skill still needs a publication warning because it scans project files and creates persistent memory and instruction files in the current workspace.

medium_findings.title: Project File Scanning and Persistent Memory Writes
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill tells the assistant to inspect project files, read memory files, and write project-local memory records. This is legitimate for a memory initializer, but it can persist sensitive project details, user roles, preferences, or decisions in the workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly direct workspace scanning and memory file creation. The behavior is expected for the skill, but the persistence of potentially sensitive content is a real risk.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Assistant Instruction Files
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill generates CLAUDE.md and optionally AGENT.md with project context, persona, style rules, and memory protocol. These files can affect future assistant behavior in the project, so users should review generated content before keeping it.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file generation behavior is directly documented. Risk depends on user-provided content and future assistant use, so confidence is high for persistence and moderate for security impact.

low_findings.title: Mostly False Positive: Markdown Command Markers
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE with one low-risk command example: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks, slash-command text, and fenced examples as shell execution. The only real shell command shown is a hardcoded mkdir command for a local memory directory with no user input interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are readable markdown instructions and templates. The hardcoded mkdir command creates a local directory and does not expose command injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hidden Home Directory Access
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The references to ~/.claude explicitly tell the assistant not to read or write that global directory. This reduces scope rather than expanding filesystem access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is an explicit prohibition against using the hidden home directory. No instruction to access hidden home files was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: No cryptographic operation, hash selection, digest generation, or weak algorithm use appears in the reviewed file. The alert appears to be triggered by ordinary markdown or file-name text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is front matter describing generated markdown files and contains no cryptographic API or algorithm. The static label does not match the semantic context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Entropy Heuristic
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The file is readable markdown with Chinese text and templates. No encoded payload, binary blob, encrypted content, or hidden executable content was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the full file showed plain markdown content. The entropy score is consistent with multilingual prose and structured templates, not obfuscation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Workspace File Read and Write Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the assistant to read project memory files and write updated memory files. This is the core feature, but it can expose or persist sensitive local context if users include secrets or confidential details.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow directly describes local file reads and writes. No exfiltration is present, so the danger is persistence and local disclosure, not remote compromise.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded Shell Directory Creation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a bash example that creates memory/. The command is hardcoded and local, so command injection risk is low, but it is still an external command pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The bash command is present and semantically clear. It does not include variables, network calls, or untrusted input.

content.value_statement: AI assistants often lose project context between sessions. This skill creates project-local memory files so Claude, Claude Code, and Codex can reuse stable context.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether CLAUDE.md, AGENT.md, MEMORY.md, or memory/ already exist.
Collects project purpose, user role, assistant usage, style preferences, and work habits.
Generates CLAUDE.md with project summary, persona, dialogue style, thinking rules, and memory protocol.
Optionally copies the same instruction content into AGENT.md for Codex workflows.
Creates an initial MEMORY.md template for long-term project memory.
Creates a memory/ directory for daily notes and optional first-session records.
limitations: It only manages files in the current project workspace.
It does not sync memory across devices or repositories.
It can store sensitive information if users provide it during setup.
It depends on future assistants following the generated memory protocol.

use_cases.title: Set up context for a new project
use_cases.description: Create reusable assistant instructions and memory files before regular project work begins.

use_cases.title: Keep client work preferences available
use_cases.description: Record stable role, communication style, customer context, and decisions in project-local memory files.

use_cases.title: Share AI workflow conventions
use_cases.description: Add CLAUDE.md or AGENT.md so team members can use consistent assistant behavior in the same repository.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Initialization
prompt_templates.scenario: Start memory setup in the current folder.
prompt_templates.prompt: Initialize memory for this project. Ask me the setup questions and create the project-local memory files.

prompt_templates.title: Claude and Codex Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Create files for both Claude Code and Codex use.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run memory init and generate both CLAUDE.md and AGENT.md if I confirm the content.

prompt_templates.title: Existing Project Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Use repository files to draft a project summary first.
prompt_templates.prompt: Initialize project memory here. Inspect the existing project files, draft a short project summary, then ask me to confirm it.

prompt_templates.title: Careful Workspace Migration
prompt_templates.scenario: Avoid overwriting existing memory or instruction files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up the memory system, but first list any existing CLAUDE.md, AGENT.md, MEMORY.md, or memory/ files and ask before changing them.

output_examples.input: Initialize memory in an empty product planning folder.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks what the project is, records the user role and style preferences, then creates project-local memory files after confirmation.

output_examples.input: Run memory init in a repository that already has a README and package metadata.
output_examples.output: The assistant drafts a short project summary from existing files, asks for edits, then prepares CLAUDE.md, MEMORY.md, and memory/.

output_examples.input: Set up memory for both Claude Code and Codex.
output_examples.output: The assistant creates CLAUDE.md and asks whether to duplicate the same instructions into AGENT.md for Codex.

best_practices: Review generated CLAUDE.md and AGENT.md before relying on them in future sessions.
Keep secrets, credentials, and private customer data out of memory files.
Use the reset or overwrite path only after checking existing files.
anti_patterns: Do not run it in a repository without checking for existing instruction files.
Do not store passwords, tokens, or confidential records in MEMORY.md.
Do not assume the memory files apply outside the current project directory.

faq.question: What files does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates CLAUDE.md, MEMORY.md, memory/, and optionally AGENT.md if the user wants Codex support.

faq.question: Does it write to global Claude memory?
faq.answer: No. The skill says to use project-local files and ignore the global ~/.claude auto-memory directory.

faq.question: Can it overwrite existing files?
faq.answer: It should ask before overwriting existing CLAUDE.md, AGENT.md, MEMORY.md, or memory/ content.

faq.question: Is this only for Claude?
faq.answer: No. It supports Claude, Claude Code, and Codex through CLAUDE.md and optional AGENT.md files.

faq.question: Does it send memory to a remote service?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access was found in the reviewed skill file.

faq.question: What should users avoid storing?
faq.answer: Users should avoid storing credentials, secrets, regulated data, or confidential customer details in memory files.
